verses.text: He sends seventy two by two; 'the harvest... is great, but the labourers are few'; pray the Lord of the harvest. A pleading prayer. The need of souls calls for more sent workers.
He sends seventy two by two; 'the harvest... is great, but the labourers are few'; pray the Lord of the harvest. A pleading prayer. The need of souls calls for more sent workers.
He sends seventy two by two; 'the harvest... is great, but the labourers are few'; pray the Lord of the harvest. A pleading prayer. The need of souls calls for more sent workers.
Carry no purse, salute none on the way; 'the labourer is worthy of his hire.' Single-minded mission. They go in haste, provided for by God.
Carry no purse, salute none on the way; 'the labourer is worthy of his hire.' Single-minded mission. They go in haste, provided for by God.

Heal the sick and say the kingdom is near; it shall be more tolerable for Sodom than the city that rejects them. Solemn weight. To refuse the gospel is graver than Sodom's sin.

Woe to Chorazin and Bethsaida; 'he that heareth you heareth me.' Identified with His own. To despise Christ's messengers is to despise Him.
Woe to Chorazin and Bethsaida; 'he that heareth you heareth me.' Identified with His own. To despise Christ's messengers is to despise Him.

The seventy return joyful that devils obey; 'rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.' Truer joy. Better to be saved than to be powerful.

He rejoices that the Father reveals to babes what is hid from the wise. Humble revelation. God opens His truth to the lowly heart.
He rejoices that the Father reveals to babes what is hid from the wise. Humble revelation. God opens His truth to the lowly heart.

A lawyer asks the way to life; love God with all thy heart and thy neighbour as thyself; 'this do, and thou shalt live.' The law's demand. Perfect love would save, but who can render it?
A lawyer asks the way to life; love God with all thy heart and thy neighbour as thyself; 'this do, and thou shalt live.' The law's demand. Perfect love would save, but who can render it?

Who is my neighbour? a wounded man is passed by the priest and Levite. Religion that fails. They had no mercy for the man in need.
Who is my neighbour? a wounded man is passed by the priest and Levite. Religion that fails. They had no mercy for the man in need.
Who is my neighbour? a wounded man is passed by the priest and Levite. Religion that fails. They had no mercy for the man in need.
Who is my neighbour? a wounded man is passed by the priest and Levite. Religion that fails. They had no mercy for the man in need.
A Samaritan, moved with compassion, binds his wounds and pays for his care. True neighbour. The despised foreigner shows the love the godly lacked.
A Samaritan, moved with compassion, binds his wounds and pays for his care. True neighbour. The despised foreigner shows the love the godly lacked.
A Samaritan, moved with compassion, binds his wounds and pays for his care. True neighbour. The despised foreigner shows the love the godly lacked.
Which was neighbour? 'He that shewed mercy.' Go, and do thou likewise. Mercy in action. Love is not a feeling but a deed.
Which was neighbour? 'He that shewed mercy.' Go, and do thou likewise. Mercy in action. Love is not a feeling but a deed.
Martha is cumbered with serving; Mary chose 'that good part,' sitting at His feet. One thing needful. To hear Christ is better than busy distraction.
